name: Firstenergy Choice
description_short: FirstEnergy Choice FCU is a federal credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with no ATM surcharges across 55,000+ locations.
description_long: FirstEnergy Choice Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative that has been serving members with deposit and lending products. The institution operates as a federally chartered credit union and provides comprehensive financial services to its membership base. FirstEnergy Choice FCU offers a full suite of consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and various loan products. They maintain an extensive ATM network with access to over 55,000 surcharge-free ATMs, positioning convenience as a core value proposition. The credit union also issues Visa credit cards with rewards programs and provides digital banking through online and mobile platforms. Loan offerings span vehicle financing (as low as 4.49% APR for cars/trucks), home equity lines of credit (5.49% APR), personal loans (9.24% APR), and other secured lending products. What distinguishes FirstEnergy Choice FCU from traditional banks is their cooperative membership structure and elimination of ATM surcharges across their network. They promote member benefits through referral bonuses (up to $100), eStatement enrollment incentives ($5 bonus), and competitive loan rates. Their service model emphasizes free digital banking, notary services, and Medallion signature guarantees. However, FirstEnergy Choice FCU appears to have a limited geographic footprint tied to FirstEnergy service territory, membership eligibility may be restricted to specific employer or community groups, and fee schedules include notable charges for overdrafts ($15), NSF ($20), wire transfers ($15-$35), and card replacements ($3-$5). The institution underwent a significant online banking platform update in March 2024, which may have created transition challenges for existing members.
services: Checking and debit card accounts with free initial Visa debit card
Savings accounts and Christmas Club savings program
Vehicle loans (car, truck, motorcycle, ATV, UTV)
Visa credit cards with ScoreCard rewards
Online and mobile banking with bill pay
pros: Access to 55,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide — members can deposit and withdraw without fees
Vehicle loan rates starting as low as 4.49% APR for car/truck financing
Home equity line of credit rates as low as 5.49% APR
Free checking account, online banking, mobile banking, and mobile deposit services
Free notary service and Medallion signature guarantees (up to 5 items monthly)
New member referral program offering up to $100 in share deposits through 6/30/2026
Credit card rewards program with ScoreCard points (6-year expiration)
cons: Membership eligibility likely restricted to FirstEnergy service territory or specific employer groups — not universally available
High overdraft ($15) and NSF fees ($20 per item) typical of credit unions
International transaction fees of 0.80%-1.50% are substantial for frequent travelers
Wire transfer fees ($15 domestic, $35 international) and check reorders add up for active users
Recent online banking platform update (3/14/2024) may create usability issues for some members
best_for: FirstEnergy employees and families in FirstEnergy service regions seeking low-cost vehicle and home equity financing
Members who value surcharge-free ATM access and prefer fee-free digital banking
Borrowers refinancing existing vehicle loans or seeking competitive rates on personal credit needs
Credit union members seeking traditional banking services with community-focused benefits and referral incentives
diagnosis: FirstEnergy Choice FCU is best for members in FirstEnergy service territories who want competitive loan rates (especially for vehicles and home equity) combined with convenience of surcharge-free ATM access and free digital banking. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is likely restricted to specific geographic areas and employer groups, making it unavailable to the general public, and fee structures for overdrafts, NSF items, and wire transfers align with typical credit union pricing rather than offering significant advantages in those areas.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
